description: Additive 'we' places the following constituent alongside an alternative already established in speech or context. It can add a participant, an object, an action, or another constituent. The conjunction 'nela' (COORD) joins grammatical equals within one expression; 'we' reaches back to what is already understood.
articulatory_notes: The lips round while the back of the tongue rises for voiced 'w'. They relax as the tongue shifts forward into true-mid 'e', and full stress covers the monosyllable.
usage_notes: At the outer edge of a Slot 2 phrase, 'we' precedes every narrower modifier. A phrase carries 'we' or 'li' (RESTR), not both. Position fixes the addition: 'we lohau wile' adds the dog, while 'ne siora we shua' adds the act of coming.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The marked constituent joins an existing set and leaves its earlier members in place. In deliberation, 'we' can make an added participant or concern explicit.
